div.h2: The Steal Policy Creates an Impossible Situation

text.tspan: But B is ALREADY on disk!

div.p: You can't "skip" something that's already on disk. The steal policy means uncommitted changes may have been written. Redo must replay them to establish a known state that Undo can then work with. Redo ≠ "replay committed" Redo = "replay to known state"

g.text: Page P still has B on disk
But what IS the current state?

text.tspan: We don't know if B is from:

div.p: Redo Principle: Replay EVERYTHING from min_rec_lsn This establishes a KNOWN state Then Undo can work correctly

g.text: Now we KNOW P = B (from T1)
